problem_text: Sam has 5 apples. He gets 3 more apples. How many apples does Sam have now?

scaffolds.L1_simplified: Sam has 5 apples. He gets 3 more. How many does he have?
scaffolds.L2_bilingual: Sam has 5 apples (manzanas). He gets 3 more (más). How many apples (manzanas) now (ahora)?
scaffolds.L3_spanish: Sam tiene 5 manzanas. Recibe 3 más. ¿Cuántas manzanas tiene ahora?

scaffolds.L1_simplified: A box has 10 crayons. A boy takes 4. How many are left?
scaffolds.L2_bilingual: A box (caja) has 10 crayons (crayones). A boy takes (toma) 4. How many (cuántos) left (quedan)?
scaffolds.L3_spanish: Una caja tiene 10 crayones. Un niño toma 4. ¿Cuántos quedan?

problem_text: A girl has 7 red balls and 2 blue balls. How many balls does she have in all?

scaffolds.L1_simplified: A girl has 7 red balls and 2 blue balls. How many total?
scaffolds.L2_bilingual: A girl (niña) has 7 red (rojas) balls (pelotas) and 2 blue (azules). How many (cuántas) in all (en total)?
scaffolds.L3_spanish: Una niña tiene 7 pelotas rojas y 2 azules. ¿Cuántas tiene en total?

problem_text: There are 12 birds on a tree. 5 birds fly away. How many birds are on the tree now?

scaffolds.L1_simplified: 12 birds are on a tree. 5 fly away. How many are left?
scaffolds.L2_bilingual: 12 birds (pájaros) are on a tree (árbol). 5 fly away (vuelan). How many (cuántos) left (quedan)?
scaffolds.L3_spanish: Hay 12 pájaros en un árbol. 5 vuelan. ¿Cuántos quedan?

problem_text: Tom has 6 toy cars. His dad gives him 4 more toy cars. How many toy cars does Tom have?

scaffolds.L1_simplified: Tom has 6 toy cars. His dad gives him 4 more. How many does he have?
scaffolds.L2_bilingual: Tom has 6 toy cars (carros de juguete). His dad (papá) gives 4 more (más). How many (cuántos)?
scaffolds.L3_spanish: Tom tiene 6 carros de juguete. Su papá le da 4 más. ¿Cuántos tiene?
scaffolds.L4_solution:
Step 1: Add — 6 + 4
Step 2: = 10
Answer: 10 toy cars.

problem_text: A bag has 15 marbles. A girl takes out 6 marbles. How many marbles are in the bag?

scaffolds.L1_simplified: A bag has 15 marbles. A girl takes out 6. How many are left?
scaffolds.L2_bilingual: A bag (bolsa) has 15 marbles (canicas). A girl takes out (saca) 6. How many (cuántas) left (quedan)?
scaffolds.L3_spanish: Una bolsa tiene 15 canicas. Una niña saca 6. ¿Cuántas quedan?

problem_text: Ana has 8 books. Her friend gives her 5 more books. How many books does Ana have now?

scaffolds.L1_simplified: Ana has 8 books. Her friend gives her 5 more. How many does she have?
scaffolds.L2_bilingual: Ana has 8 books (libros). Her friend (amiga) gives 5 more (más). How many books (libros) now (ahora)?
scaffolds.L3_spanish: Ana tiene 8 libros. Su amiga le da 5 más. ¿Cuántos libros tiene ahora?

problem_text: There are 20 cookies on a plate. Kids eat 9 cookies. How many cookies are left?

scaffolds.L1_simplified: There are 20 cookies. Kids eat 9. How many are left?
scaffolds.L2_bilingual: There are 20 cookies (galletas). Kids (niños) eat (comen) 9. How many (cuántas) left (quedan)?
scaffolds.L3_spanish: Hay 20 galletas. Los niños comen 9. ¿Cuántas quedan?

problem_text: A pet store has 11 cats and 7 dogs. How many animals are in the store?

scaffolds.L1_simplified: A store has 11 cats and 7 dogs. How many animals total?
scaffolds.L2_bilingual: A store (tienda) has 11 cats (gatos) and 7 dogs (perros). How many animals (animales) total (en total)?
scaffolds.L3_spanish: Una tienda tiene 11 gatos y 7 perros. ¿Cuántos animales hay?

problem_text: A boy has 14 stickers. He gives 5 stickers to his sister. How many stickers does he have left?

scaffolds.L1_simplified: A boy has 14 stickers. He gives 5 to his sister. How many does he have left?
scaffolds.L2_bilingual: A boy (niño) has 14 stickers (calcomanías). He gives (da) 5 to his sister (hermana). How many (cuántas) left (quedan)?
scaffolds.L3_spanish: Un niño tiene 14 calcomanías. Le da 5 a su hermana. ¿Cuántas le quedan?

problem_text: A bag has 4 apples. There are 3 bags. How many apples are there in all?

scaffolds.L1_simplified: Each bag has 4 apples. There are 3 bags. How many apples total?
scaffolds.L2_bilingual: Each bag (bolsa) has 4 apples (manzanas). There are 3 bags. How many apples (manzanas) in all (en total)?
scaffolds.L3_spanish: Cada bolsa tiene 4 manzanas. Hay 3 bolsas. ¿Cuántas manzanas hay en total?

problem_text: There are 12 cookies. They are shared equally among 4 children. How many cookies does each child get?

scaffolds.L1_simplified: 12 cookies are shared equally among 4 children. How many does each child get?
scaffolds.L2_bilingual: 12 cookies (galletas) are shared equally (igualmente) among 4 children (niños). How many (cuántas) does each (cada uno) get?
scaffolds.L3_spanish: Hay 12 galletas. Se reparten entre 4 niños por igual. ¿Cuántas recibe cada niño?
scaffolds.L4_solution:
Step 1: Divide — 12 ÷ 4
Step 2: = 3
Answer: 3 cookies each.

problem_text: A box holds 6 cans. There are 5 boxes. How many cans are there in total?

scaffolds.L1_simplified: Each box holds 6 cans. There are 5 boxes. How many cans total?
scaffolds.L2_bilingual: Each box (caja) holds (tiene) 6 cans (latas). There are 5 boxes. How many cans (latas) total?
scaffolds.L3_spanish: Cada caja tiene 6 latas. Hay 5 cajas. ¿Cuántas latas hay en total?

problem_text: A teacher has 20 pencils. She gives the same number to each of 4 students. How many pencils does each student get?

scaffolds.L1_simplified: A teacher has 20 pencils. She gives equal amounts to 4 students. How many does each get?
scaffolds.L2_bilingual: A teacher (maestra) has 20 pencils (lápices). She gives equal (iguales) amounts to 4 students (estudiantes). How many (cuántos) each?
scaffolds.L3_spanish: Una maestra tiene 20 lápices. Los reparte entre 4 estudiantes por igual. ¿Cuántos recibe cada uno?
scaffolds.L4_solution:
Step 1: Divide — 20 ÷ 4
Step 2: = 5
Answer: 5 pencils each.

problem_text: A carton has 8 eggs. There are 3 cartons. How many eggs are there altogether?

scaffolds.L1_simplified: Each carton has 8 eggs. There are 3 cartons. How many eggs altogether?
scaffolds.L2_bilingual: Each carton (cartón) has 8 eggs (huevos). There are 3 cartons. How many eggs (huevos) altogether (en total)?
scaffolds.L3_spanish: Cada cartón tiene 8 huevos. Hay 3 cartones. ¿Cuántos huevos hay en total?

problem_text: There are 18 balloons. They are divided equally into 6 groups. How many balloons are in each group?

scaffolds.L1_simplified: 18 balloons are divided into 6 equal groups. How many in each group?
scaffolds.L2_bilingual: 18 balloons (globos) are divided (divididos) into 6 equal (iguales) groups (grupos). How many (cuántos) in each group (cada grupo)?
scaffolds.L3_spanish: Hay 18 globos divididos en 6 grupos iguales. ¿Cuántos hay en cada grupo?
scaffolds.L4_solution:
Step 1: Divide — 18 ÷ 6
Step 2: = 3
Answer: 3 balloons each.

problem_text: A shelf has 7 books. There are 4 shelves. How many books are there in all?

scaffolds.L1_simplified: Each shelf has 7 books. There are 4 shelves. How many books total?
scaffolds.L2_bilingual: Each shelf (estante) has 7 books (libros). There are 4 shelves. How many books (libros) in all (en total)?
scaffolds.L3_spanish: Cada estante tiene 7 libros. Hay 4 estantes. ¿Cuántos libros hay en total?

problem_text: A farmer has 24 carrots. He puts them into bags of 8. How many bags does he fill?

scaffolds.L1_simplified: A farmer has 24 carrots. He puts 8 in each bag. How many bags?
scaffolds.L2_bilingual: A farmer (granjero) has 24 carrots (zanahorias). He puts 8 in each bag (bolsa). How many bags (bolsas)?
scaffolds.L3_spanish: Un granjero tiene 24 zanahorias. Pone 8 en cada bolsa. ¿Cuántas bolsas llena?

problem_text: Each child gets 5 candies. There are 6 children at the party. How many candies are given out in all?

scaffolds.L1_simplified: Each child gets 5 candies. There are 6 children. How many candies total?
scaffolds.L2_bilingual: Each child (niño) gets 5 candies (dulces). There are 6 children at the party (fiesta). How many candies (dulces) total?
scaffolds.L3_spanish: Cada niño recibe 5 dulces. Hay 6 niños en la fiesta. ¿Cuántos dulces se dan en total?

problem_text: There are 30 students in a class. They sit in rows of 5. How many rows are there?

scaffolds.L1_simplified: 30 students sit in rows of 5. How many rows are there?
scaffolds.L2_bilingual: 30 students (estudiantes) sit in rows (filas) of 5. How many rows (filas)?
scaffolds.L3_spanish: Hay 30 estudiantes sentados en filas de 5. ¿Cuántas filas hay?

problem_text: Maria has 10 dollars. She buys a book for 4 dollars. How much money does Maria have left?

scaffolds.L1_simplified: Maria has 10 dollars. She spends 4 dollars on a book. How much is left?
scaffolds.L2_bilingual: Maria has 10 dollars (dólares). She buys (compra) a book (libro) for 4 dollars. How much money (dinero) is left (le queda)?
scaffolds.L3_spanish: María tiene 10 dólares. Compra un libro por 4 dólares. ¿Cuánto dinero le queda?

problem_text: A shop sells 15 sandwiches in the morning and 12 sandwiches in the afternoon. How many sandwiches are sold in all?

scaffolds.L1_simplified: A shop sells 15 sandwiches in the morning and 12 in the afternoon. How many in all?
scaffolds.L2_bilingual: A shop (tienda) sells (vende) 15 sandwiches in the morning (mañana) and 12 in the afternoon (tarde). How many (cuántos) in all (en total)?
scaffolds.L3_spanish: Una tienda vende 15 sándwiches en la mañana y 12 en la tarde. ¿Cuántos vende en total?

problem_text: Jake buys a drink for 2 dollars and a snack for 3 dollars. He pays with a 10-dollar bill. How much change does he get?

scaffolds.L1_simplified: Jake buys a drink for 2 dollars and a snack for 3 dollars. He pays 10 dollars. How much change?
scaffolds.L2_bilingual: Jake buys (compra) a drink (bebida) for 2 dollars and a snack (merienda) for 3 dollars. He pays (paga) 10 dollars. How much change (cambio)?
scaffolds.L3_spanish: Jake compra una bebida por 2 dólares y una merienda por 3 dólares. Paga con un billete de 10. ¿Cuánto cambio recibe?
scaffolds.L4_solution:
Step 1: Total cost = 2 + 3 = 5
Step 2: Change = 10 - 5 = 5
Answer: 5 dollars change.

problem_text: A movie starts at 3 o'clock and lasts for 2 hours. What time does the movie end?

scaffolds.L1_simplified: A movie starts at 3 o'clock. It lasts 2 hours. What time does it end?
scaffolds.L2_bilingual: A movie (película) starts (empieza) at 3 o'clock and lasts (dura) 2 hours (horas). What time (a qué hora) does it end (termina)?
scaffolds.L3_spanish: Una película empieza a las 3 en punto y dura 2 horas. ¿A qué hora termina?

problem_text: A pencil costs 25 cents and an eraser costs 15 cents. How much do they cost together?

scaffolds.L1_simplified: A pencil costs 25 cents. An eraser costs 15 cents. How much together?
scaffolds.L2_bilingual: A pencil (lápiz) costs (cuesta) 25 cents (centavos) and an eraser (borrador) costs 15 cents. How much (cuánto) together (juntos)?
scaffolds.L3_spanish: Un lápiz cuesta 25 centavos y un borrador 15 centavos. ¿Cuánto cuestan juntos?

problem_text: There are 30 students in a class. 12 are girls. How many boys are in the class?

scaffolds.L1_simplified: A class has 30 students. 12 are girls. How many are boys?
scaffolds.L2_bilingual: A class (clase) has 30 students (estudiantes). 12 are girls (niñas). How many are boys (niños)?
scaffolds.L3_spanish: Hay 30 estudiantes en una clase. 12 son niñas. ¿Cuántos son niños?

problem_text: School starts at 8 in the morning and ends at 3 in the afternoon. How many hours is the school day?

scaffolds.L1_simplified: School starts at 8 AM and ends at 3 PM. How many hours is the school day?
scaffolds.L2_bilingual: School (escuela) starts (empieza) at 8 AM and ends (termina) at 3 PM. How many hours (horas) is the school day (día escolar)?
scaffolds.L3_spanish: La escuela empieza a las 8 AM y termina a las 3 PM. ¿Cuántas horas dura el día escolar?
scaffolds.L4_solution:
Step 1: Count from 8 AM to 3 PM
Step 2: = 7 hours
Answer: 7 hours.

problem_text: A rope is 20 feet long. A girl cuts off 7 feet. How long is the rope now?

scaffolds.L1_simplified: A rope is 20 feet long. A girl cuts 7 feet off. How long is it now?
scaffolds.L2_bilingual: A rope (cuerda) is 20 feet (pies) long. A girl cuts off (corta) 7 feet. How long (cuánto mide) now?
scaffolds.L3_spanish: Una cuerda mide 20 pies. Una niña corta 7 pies. ¿Cuánto mide ahora?

problem_text: Luis saves 5 dollars each week. How much money will he save in 4 weeks?

scaffolds.L1_simplified: Luis saves 5 dollars each week for 4 weeks. How much does he save in all?
scaffolds.L2_bilingual: Luis saves (ahorra) 5 dollars (dólares) each week (semana). How much (cuánto) in 4 weeks (semanas)?
scaffolds.L3_spanish: Luis ahorra 5 dólares cada semana. ¿Cuánto ahorrará en 4 semanas?

problem_text: A garden is 8 meters long and 3 meters wide. What is the area of the garden?

scaffolds.L1_simplified: A garden is 8 meters long and 3 meters wide. What is the area?
scaffolds.L2_bilingual: A garden (jardín) is 8 meters (metros) long (de largo) and 3 meters wide (de ancho). What is the area (área)?
scaffolds.L3_spanish: Un jardín mide 8 metros de largo y 3 de ancho. ¿Cuál es el área?
scaffolds.L4_solution:
Step 1: Multiply — 8 × 3
Step 2: = 24
Answer: 24 square meters.

problem_text: John has 24 trading cards. His brother has 16 cards. How many more cards does John have than his brother?

scaffolds.L1_simplified: John has 24 cards. His brother has 16. How many more does John have?
scaffolds.L2_bilingual: John has 24 trading cards (tarjetas). His brother (hermano) has 16. How many more (cuántas más)?
scaffolds.L3_spanish: John tiene 24 tarjetas. Su hermano tiene 16. ¿Cuántas más tiene John?
scaffolds.L4_solution:
Step 1: Subtract — 24 - 16
Step 2: = 8
Answer: 8 more cards.

problem_text: A blue car traveled 180 miles. A red car traveled 145 miles. How much farther did the blue car travel?
solution_steps: The blue car traveled 35 miles farther.

scaffolds.L1_simplified: A blue car went 180 miles. A red car went 145 miles. How much farther did the blue car go?
scaffolds.L2_bilingual: A blue car (carro azul) traveled (viajó) 180 miles (millas). A red car (carro rojo) traveled 145. How much farther (cuánto más lejos)?
scaffolds.L3_spanish: Un carro azul viajó 180 millas. Un carro rojo viajó 145 millas. ¿Cuánto más lejos viajó el azul?
scaffolds.L4_solution:
Step 1: Subtract — 180 - 145
Step 2: = 35
Answer: 35 miles farther.

problem_text: A store had 50 shirts. They sold 28 on Monday and 13 on Tuesday. How many shirts are left?

scaffolds.L1_simplified: A store had 50 shirts. They sold 28 on Monday and 13 on Tuesday. How many are left?
scaffolds.L2_bilingual: A store (tienda) had 50 shirts (camisas). They sold (vendieron) 28 on Monday (lunes) and 13 on Tuesday (martes). How many left (quedan)?
scaffolds.L3_spanish: Una tienda tenía 50 camisas. Vendieron 28 el lunes y 13 el martes. ¿Cuántas quedan?
scaffolds.L4_solution:
Step 1: Sold = 28 + 13 = 41
Step 2: Left = 50 - 41 = 9
Answer: 9 shirts.

problem_text: A ribbon is 3 meters long. Rosa cuts it into pieces that are each 50 centimeters long. How many pieces can she cut?

scaffolds.L1_simplified: A ribbon is 3 meters long. Rosa cuts it into 50-centimeter pieces. How many pieces?
scaffolds.L2_bilingual: A ribbon (cinta) is 3 meters (metros) long. Rosa cuts (corta) it into pieces (piezas) of 50 centimeters (centímetros). How many pieces (piezas)?
scaffolds.L3_spanish: Una cinta mide 3 metros. Rosa la corta en piezas de 50 centímetros. ¿Cuántas piezas obtiene?
scaffolds.L4_solution:
Step 1: Convert — 3 m = 300 cm
Step 2: Divide — 300 ÷ 50 = 6
Answer: 6 pieces.

problem_text: Elena scored 85 points on her first test and 92 points on her second test. How many more points did she score on the second test?

scaffolds.L1_simplified: Elena scored 85 on the first test and 92 on the second. How many more points on the second?
scaffolds.L2_bilingual: Elena scored (obtuvo) 85 points (puntos) on her first test (primer examen) and 92 on the second (segundo). How many more (cuántos más)?
scaffolds.L3_spanish: Elena obtuvo 85 puntos en el primer examen y 92 en el segundo. ¿Cuántos puntos más en el segundo?
scaffolds.L4_solution:
Step 1: Subtract — 92 - 85
Step 2: = 7
Answer: 7 more points.

problem_text: A train leaves at 9:15 AM and arrives at 11:45 AM. How long is the train ride?

scaffolds.L1_simplified: A train leaves at 9:15 AM. It arrives at 11:45 AM. How long is the ride?
scaffolds.L2_bilingual: A train (tren) leaves (sale) at 9:15 AM and arrives (llega) at 11:45 AM. How long (cuánto tiempo) is the ride (viaje)?
scaffolds.L3_spanish: Un tren sale a las 9:15 AM y llega a las 11:45 AM. ¿Cuánto dura el viaje?
scaffolds.L4_solution:
Step 1: 11:45 - 9:15
Step 2: = 2 hours 30 minutes
Answer: 2 hours 30 minutes.

problem_text: A box of 24 chocolates costs 6 dollars. How much does each chocolate cost?

scaffolds.L1_simplified: 24 chocolates cost 6 dollars. How much does each chocolate cost?
scaffolds.L2_bilingual: A box (caja) of 24 chocolates (chocolates) costs (cuesta) 6 dollars (dólares). How much (cuánto) does each (cada uno) cost?
scaffolds.L3_spanish: Una caja de 24 chocolates cuesta 6 dólares. ¿Cuánto cuesta cada chocolate?
scaffolds.L4_solution:
Step 1: 6 dollars = 600 cents
Step 2: 600 ÷ 24 = 25
Answer: 25 cents each.

problem_text: Team A scored 47 points and Team B scored 39 points. How many more points did Team A score?

scaffolds.L1_simplified: Team A scored 47 points. Team B scored 39. How many more did Team A score?
scaffolds.L2_bilingual: Team A scored (anotó) 47 points (puntos). Team B scored 39. How many more (cuántos más) did Team A score?
scaffolds.L3_spanish: El Equipo A anotó 47 puntos y el Equipo B 39. ¿Cuántos puntos más anotó el Equipo A?
scaffolds.L4_solution:
Step 1: Subtract — 47 - 39
Step 2: = 8
Answer: 8 more points.

problem_text: A swimming pool is 25 meters long. Carlos swam 4 lengths of the pool. How many meters did he swim?

scaffolds.L1_simplified: A pool is 25 meters long. Carlos swam 4 lengths. How many meters total?
scaffolds.L2_bilingual: A pool (piscina) is 25 meters (metros) long. Carlos swam (nadó) 4 lengths (largos). How many meters (metros) total?
scaffolds.L3_spanish: Una piscina mide 25 metros. Carlos nadó 4 largos. ¿Cuántos metros nadó en total?

problem_text: A family buys 3 movie tickets for 8 dollars each. How much do they pay in total?

scaffolds.L1_simplified: A family buys 3 movie tickets. Each costs 8 dollars. How much total?
scaffolds.L2_bilingual: A family (familia) buys (compra) 3 movie tickets (boletos de cine). Each (cada uno) costs 8 dollars. Total (total)?
scaffolds.L3_spanish: Una familia compra 3 boletos de cine a 8 dólares cada uno. ¿Cuánto pagan en total?

problem_text: A pizza is cut into 8 equal slices. Maya eats 3 slices. What fraction of the pizza did Maya eat?

scaffolds.L1_simplified: A pizza has 8 slices. Maya eats 3. What fraction did she eat?
scaffolds.L2_bilingual: A pizza has 8 equal slices (rebanadas). Maya eats (come) 3. What fraction (fracción) did she eat?
scaffolds.L3_spanish: Una pizza está cortada en 8 rebanadas iguales. Maya come 3. ¿Qué fracción de la pizza comió?
scaffolds.L4_solution:
Step 1: Eaten = 3
Step 2: Total = 8
Step 3: Fraction = 3/8
Answer: 3/8

problem_text: There are 12 students in a group. One-third of them are wearing glasses. How many students wear glasses?

scaffolds.L1_simplified: There are 12 students. One-third wear glasses. How many wear glasses?
scaffolds.L2_bilingual: There are 12 students (estudiantes). One-third (un tercio) wear glasses (usan lentes). How many (cuántos)?
scaffolds.L3_spanish: Hay 12 estudiantes. Un tercio usa lentes. ¿Cuántos estudiantes usan lentes?
scaffolds.L4_solution:
Step 1: Divide by 3 — 12 ÷ 3
Step 2: = 4
Answer: 4 students.

problem_text: A baker makes 5 dozen cookies. He sells 36 cookies. How many cookies does he have left?

scaffolds.L1_simplified: A baker makes 5 dozen (60) cookies. He sells 36. How many are left?
scaffolds.L2_bilingual: A baker (panadero) makes 5 dozen (docenas) cookies (galletas). He sells (vende) 36. How many (cuántas) left (quedan)?
scaffolds.L3_spanish: Un panadero hace 5 docenas de galletas. Vende 36. ¿Cuántas le quedan?

problem_text: David reads 1/4 of his book on Monday and 1/4 on Tuesday. What fraction of the book has he read in total?

scaffolds.L1_simplified: David reads 1/4 of his book on Monday and 1/4 on Tuesday. What fraction total?
scaffolds.L2_bilingual: David reads (lee) 1/4 of his book (libro) on Monday (lunes) and 1/4 on Tuesday (martes). What fraction (fracción) total?
scaffolds.L3_spanish: David lee 1/4 de su libro el lunes y 1/4 el martes. ¿Qué fracción ha leído en total?

problem_text: A store sells apples at 3 for a dollar. How much will 15 apples cost?

scaffolds.L1_simplified: 3 apples cost 1 dollar. How much do 15 apples cost?
scaffolds.L2_bilingual: Apples (manzanas) cost 3 for a dollar (dólar). How much (cuánto) do 15 apples cost?
scaffolds.L3_spanish: Las manzanas cuestan 3 por un dólar. ¿Cuánto cuestan 15 manzanas?

problem_text: A jar has 20 marbles. Half are red and one-quarter are blue. How many marbles are neither red nor blue?

scaffolds.L1_simplified: A jar has 20 marbles. Half are red. One-quarter are blue. How many are neither?
scaffolds.L2_bilingual: A jar (frasco) has 20 marbles (canicas). Half (la mitad) are red (rojas). One-quarter (un cuarto) are blue (azules). How many are neither (ni uno ni otro)?
scaffolds.L3_spanish: Un frasco tiene 20 canicas. La mitad son rojas y un cuarto son azules. ¿Cuántas no son ni rojas ni azules?
scaffolds.L4_solution:
Step 1: Red = 10, Blue = 5
Step 2: 20 - 10 - 5 = 5
Answer: 5 marbles.

problem_text: A plant grows 2 centimeters each week. How tall will it be after 6 weeks if it starts at 5 centimeters?

scaffolds.L1_simplified: A plant starts at 5 cm tall. It grows 2 cm each week. How tall after 6 weeks?
scaffolds.L2_bilingual: A plant (planta) grows (crece) 2 centimeters (centímetros) each week (semana). It starts at 5 cm. How tall (cuánto mide) after 6 weeks?
scaffolds.L3_spanish: Una planta crece 2 centímetros cada semana y empieza midiendo 5 cm. ¿Cuánto medirá después de 6 semanas?
scaffolds.L4_solution:
Step 1: Growth = 2 × 6 = 12 cm
Step 2: Total = 5 + 12 = 17
Answer: 17 cm.

problem_text: A class of 24 students went on a field trip. Three-eighths brought their lunch. How many students brought lunch?

scaffolds.L1_simplified: 24 students went on a trip. Three-eighths brought lunch. How many brought lunch?
scaffolds.L2_bilingual: 24 students (estudiantes) went on a field trip (excursión). Three-eighths (tres octavos) brought their lunch (almuerzo). How many (cuántos)?
scaffolds.L3_spanish: 24 estudiantes fueron de excursión. Tres octavos llevaron su almuerzo. ¿Cuántos lo llevaron?

problem_text: A car uses 2 gallons of gas for every 40 miles. How many gallons does it need to travel 120 miles?

scaffolds.L1_simplified: A car uses 2 gallons for every 40 miles. How many gallons for 120 miles?
scaffolds.L2_bilingual: A car (carro) uses (usa) 2 gallons (galones) for every 40 miles (millas). How many gallons (galones) for 120 miles?
scaffolds.L3_spanish: Un carro usa 2 galones por cada 40 millas. ¿Cuántos galones necesita para 120 millas?

problem_text: A rectangle has a length of 10 cm and a width of 6 cm. What is the perimeter of the rectangle?

scaffolds.L1_simplified: A rectangle is 10 cm long and 6 cm wide. What is the perimeter?
scaffolds.L2_bilingual: A rectangle (rectángulo) has length (largo) 10 cm and width (ancho) 6 cm. What is the perimeter (perímetro)?
scaffolds.L3_spanish: Un rectángulo tiene 10 cm de largo y 6 cm de ancho. ¿Cuál es el perímetro?
scaffolds.L4_solution:
Step 1: P = 2 × (l + w)
Step 2: = 2 × 16 = 32
Answer: 32 cm.

problem_text: A recipe calls for 2/3 cup of sugar and 1/4 cup of butter. How much more sugar than butter does the recipe need?

scaffolds.L1_simplified: A recipe needs 2/3 cup of sugar and 1/4 cup of butter. How much more sugar than butter?
scaffolds.L2_bilingual: A recipe (receta) calls for (necesita) 2/3 cup (taza) of sugar (azúcar) and 1/4 cup of butter (mantequilla). How much more (cuánto más)?
scaffolds.L3_spanish: Una receta necesita 2/3 de taza de azúcar y 1/4 de taza de mantequilla. ¿Cuánto más azúcar que mantequilla?

problem_text: A store sells apples for $1.25 each and oranges for $0.85 each. What is the total cost of 3 apples and 2 oranges?

scaffolds.L1_simplified: Apples cost $1.25 each. Oranges cost $0.85 each. What is the total for 3 apples and 2 oranges?
scaffolds.L2_bilingual: Apples (manzanas) cost $1.25 each and oranges (naranjas) cost $0.85 each. Total cost (costo total) of 3 apples and 2 oranges?
scaffolds.L3_spanish: Las manzanas cuestan $1.25 y las naranjas $0.85. ¿Cuánto cuestan 3 manzanas y 2 naranjas?
scaffolds.L4_solution:
Step 1: Apples = 3 × $1.25 = $3.75
Step 2: Oranges = 2 × $0.85 = $1.70
Step 3: Total = $5.45
Answer: $5.45

problem_text: Marco spent 3/5 of his allowance on lunch and 1/5 on a pen. What fraction of his allowance did he spend in total?

scaffolds.L1_simplified: Marco spent 3/5 of his money on lunch and 1/5 on a pen. How much in total?
scaffolds.L2_bilingual: Marco spent (gastó) 3/5 of his allowance (mesada) on lunch (almuerzo) and 1/5 on a pen (pluma). Total fraction (fracción total)?
scaffolds.L3_spanish: Marco gastó 3/5 de su mesada en el almuerzo y 1/5 en un bolígrafo. ¿Qué fracción gastó en total?

problem_text: A jacket costs $48.75. It is on sale for $12.50 off. What is the sale price?

scaffolds.L1_simplified: A jacket costs $48.75. There is a $12.50 discount. What is the new price?
scaffolds.L2_bilingual: A jacket (chaqueta) costs $48.75. It is on sale (en oferta) for $12.50 off (de descuento). What is the sale price (precio de oferta)?
scaffolds.L3_spanish: Una chaqueta cuesta $48.75 y tiene un descuento de $12.50. ¿Cuál es el precio de oferta?

problem_text: A board is 4 and 1/2 feet long. A carpenter cuts off 1 and 3/4 feet. How long is the board now?

scaffolds.L1_simplified: A board is 4 and 1/2 feet long. A carpenter cuts off 1 and 3/4 feet. How long is it now?
scaffolds.L2_bilingual: A board (tabla) is 4 and 1/2 feet (pies) long. A carpenter (carpintero) cuts off (corta) 1 and 3/4 feet. How long (cuánto mide) now?
scaffolds.L3_spanish: Una tabla mide 4 y 1/2 pies. Un carpintero corta 1 y 3/4 pies. ¿Cuánto mide ahora?

problem_text: Three friends share the cost of a pizza equally. The pizza costs $18.60. How much does each person pay?

scaffolds.L1_simplified: Three friends share a $18.60 pizza equally. How much does each pay?
scaffolds.L2_bilingual: Three friends (amigos) share (comparten) the cost (costo) equally. The pizza costs $18.60. How much does each person (persona) pay?
scaffolds.L3_spanish: Tres amigos comparten el costo de una pizza de $18.60 por igual. ¿Cuánto paga cada uno?

problem_text: A school has 240 students. Two-fifths of the students play after-school sports. How many students play sports?

scaffolds.L1_simplified: A school has 240 students. Two-fifths play after-school sports. How many play sports?
scaffolds.L2_bilingual: A school (escuela) has 240 students (estudiantes). Two-fifths (dos quintos) play after-school sports (deportes). How many students (cuántos)?
scaffolds.L3_spanish: Una escuela tiene 240 estudiantes. Dos quintos participan en deportes extraescolares. ¿Cuántos estudiantes practican deportes?

problem_text: A runner completed a 5-kilometer race in 32.5 minutes. What was her average time per kilometer?

scaffolds.L1_simplified: A runner ran 5 km in 32.5 minutes. What was her average time per kilometer?
scaffolds.L2_bilingual: A runner (corredora) completed (completó) a 5-kilometer race (carrera) in 32.5 minutes. What was the average time (tiempo promedio) per kilometer?
scaffolds.L3_spanish: Una corredora terminó una carrera de 5 km en 32.5 minutos. ¿Cuál fue su tiempo promedio por kilómetro?

problem_text: A bag of flour weighs 3 and 1/2 pounds. A baker uses 2 and 3/4 pounds. How much flour is left?

scaffolds.L1_simplified: A bag of flour weighs 3 and 1/2 pounds. A baker uses 2 and 3/4 pounds. How much is left?
scaffolds.L2_bilingual: A bag (bolsa) of flour (harina) weighs (pesa) 3 and 1/2 pounds (libras). A baker (panadero) uses 2 and 3/4 pounds. How much left (cuánta queda)?
scaffolds.L3_spanish: Una bolsa de harina pesa 3 y 1/2 libras. Un panadero usa 2 y 3/4 libras. ¿Cuánta harina queda?

problem_text: A water tank holds 150.5 liters. After watering a garden, there are 87.25 liters left. How much water was used?

scaffolds.L1_simplified: A tank holds 150.5 liters. After watering, 87.25 liters remain. How much water was used?
scaffolds.L2_bilingual: A tank (tanque) holds (contiene) 150.5 liters (litros). After watering (regar), 87.25 liters are left. How much water (agua) was used (se usó)?
scaffolds.L3_spanish: Un tanque tiene 150.5 litros. Después de regar el jardín, quedan 87.25 litros. ¿Cuánta agua se usó?

problem_text: A shirt originally costs $40. It is on sale for 25% off. What is the sale price?

scaffolds.L1_simplified: A shirt costs $40. It has a 25% discount. What is the sale price?
scaffolds.L2_bilingual: A shirt (camisa) costs $40. It is on sale (en oferta) for 25% off (de descuento). What is the sale price (precio de oferta)?
scaffolds.L3_spanish: Una camisa cuesta $40 y tiene un descuento del 25%. ¿Cuál es el precio de oferta?

problem_text: A town had a population of 8,000 last year. This year the population increased by 15%. What is the new population?

scaffolds.L1_simplified: A town had 8,000 people. The population grew by 15%. How many people now?
scaffolds.L2_bilingual: A town (pueblo) had a population (población) of 8,000 last year. It increased (aumentó) by 15%. What is the new population (población nueva)?
scaffolds.L3_spanish: Un pueblo tenía 8,000 habitantes. Este año aumentó un 15%. ¿Cuál es la nueva población?

problem_text: A student answered 36 out of 45 questions correctly on a test. What percentage did the student get correct?

scaffolds.L1_simplified: A student got 36 out of 45 questions right. What percentage is correct?
scaffolds.L2_bilingual: A student (estudiante) answered 36 out of 45 questions (preguntas) correctly (correctamente). What percentage (porcentaje)?
scaffolds.L3_spanish: Un estudiante respondió correctamente 36 de 45 preguntas. ¿Qué porcentaje respondió bien?

problem_text: A bicycle originally costs $320. The price is reduced by 20%. What is the new price?

scaffolds.L1_simplified: A bicycle costs $320. The price goes down by 20%. What is the new price?
scaffolds.L2_bilingual: A bicycle (bicicleta) costs $320. The price is reduced (reducido) by 20%. What is the new price (precio nuevo)?
scaffolds.L3_spanish: Una bicicleta cuesta $320. El precio baja un 20%. ¿Cuál es el nuevo precio?

problem_text: If 5 notebooks cost $12.50, how much would 8 notebooks cost at the same price per notebook?

scaffolds.L1_simplified: 5 notebooks cost $12.50. How much do 8 notebooks cost at the same price?
scaffolds.L2_bilingual: If 5 notebooks (cuadernos) cost $12.50, how much (cuánto) would 8 notebooks cost at the same price (mismo precio)?
scaffolds.L3_spanish: Si 5 cuadernos cuestan $12.50, ¿cuánto costarían 8 cuadernos al mismo precio?

problem_text: A restaurant bill is $55.00. A customer wants to leave a 20% tip. What is the tip, and what is the total amount?

scaffolds.L1_simplified: A restaurant bill is $55. The customer leaves a 20% tip. What is the tip and the total?
scaffolds.L2_bilingual: A restaurant bill (cuenta) is $55. A customer (cliente) leaves a 20% tip (propina). How much is the tip and the total (total)?
scaffolds.L3_spanish: La cuenta del restaurante es $55. El cliente deja una propina del 20%. ¿Cuánto es la propina y el total?

problem_text: A car travels 150 miles in 3 hours. At the same speed, how far will it travel in 5 hours?

scaffolds.L1_simplified: A car goes 150 miles in 3 hours. How far does it go in 5 hours at the same speed?
scaffolds.L2_bilingual: A car (carro) travels (viaja) 150 miles (millas) in 3 hours (horas). At the same speed (velocidad), how far (cuánto) in 5 hours?
scaffolds.L3_spanish: Un carro recorre 150 millas en 3 horas. A la misma velocidad, ¿cuántas millas recorrerá en 5 horas?
scaffolds.L4_solution:
Step 1: Speed = 150 ÷ 3 = 50 mph
Step 2: Distance = 50 × 5 = 250
Answer: 250 miles.

problem_text: In a class of 30 students, 40% prefer reading to watching TV. How many students prefer reading?

scaffolds.L1_simplified: A class has 30 students. 40% prefer reading over watching TV. How many prefer reading?
scaffolds.L2_bilingual: In a class (clase) of 30 students (estudiantes), 40% prefer (prefieren) reading (leer) over watching TV (ver TV). How many (cuántos)?
scaffolds.L3_spanish: En una clase de 30 estudiantes, el 40% prefiere leer sobre ver televisión. ¿Cuántos prefieren leer?

problem_text: A map has a scale of 1 inch to 20 miles. Two cities are 3.5 inches apart on the map. What is the actual distance?

scaffolds.L1_simplified: A map uses 1 inch = 20 miles. Two cities are 3.5 inches apart. How far are they really?
scaffolds.L2_bilingual: A map (mapa) has a scale (escala) of 1 inch (pulgada) = 20 miles (millas). Two cities (ciudades) are 3.5 inches apart. Actual distance (distancia real)?
scaffolds.L3_spanish: Un mapa tiene escala 1 pulgada = 20 millas. Dos ciudades están a 3.5 pulgadas en el mapa. ¿Cuál es la distancia real?
scaffolds.L4_solution:
Step 1: 1 inch = 20 miles
Step 2: 3.5 × 20 = 70
Answer: 70 miles.

problem_text: A worker earns $2,400 per month. She gets a 5% raise. What will her new monthly salary be?

scaffolds.L1_simplified: A worker earns $2,400 per month. She gets a 5% raise. What is her new salary?
scaffolds.L2_bilingual: A worker (trabajadora) earns (gana) $2,400 per month (por mes). She gets a 5% raise (aumento). New salary (salario nuevo)?
scaffolds.L3_spanish: Una trabajadora gana $2,400 por mes. Recibe un aumento del 5%. ¿Cuál será su nuevo salario?

problem_text: A car travels 240 miles using 8 gallons of gas. What is the car's fuel efficiency in miles per gallon?

scaffolds.L1_simplified: A car travels 240 miles and uses 8 gallons of gas. How many miles per gallon?
scaffolds.L2_bilingual: A car (carro) travels (viaja) 240 miles (millas) using 8 gallons (galones) of gas (gasolina). What is the fuel efficiency (eficiencia de combustible)?
scaffolds.L3_spanish: Un carro recorre 240 millas usando 8 galones. ¿Cuántas millas por galón rinde?

problem_text: A grocery store sells 6 cans of soup for $4.50. What is the price per can? How much would 10 cans cost?

scaffolds.L1_simplified: 6 cans of soup cost $4.50. What is the price per can? How much do 10 cans cost?
scaffolds.L2_bilingual: A store sells 6 cans (latas) of soup (sopa) for $4.50. What is the price per can (precio por lata)? How much (cuánto) for 10 cans?
scaffolds.L3_spanish: Una tienda vende 6 latas de sopa por $4.50. ¿Cuál es el precio por lata? ¿Cuánto costarían 10 latas?

problem_text: A swimming pool contains 12,000 liters of water. It drains at a rate of 500 liters per hour. How long will it take to empty the pool?

scaffolds.L1_simplified: A pool has 12,000 liters. It drains at 500 liters per hour. How many hours to empty it?
scaffolds.L2_bilingual: A pool (piscina) has 12,000 liters (litros). It drains (se vacía) at 500 liters per hour (por hora). How long (cuánto tiempo) to empty (vaciarse)?
scaffolds.L3_spanish: Una piscina tiene 12,000 litros. Se vacía a 500 litros por hora. ¿Cuánto tiempo tarda en vaciarse?

problem_text: The ratio of boys to girls in a club is 3:5. If there are 24 girls, how many boys are there?

scaffolds.L1_simplified: The ratio of boys to girls is 3:5. There are 24 girls. How many boys?
scaffolds.L2_bilingual: The ratio (razón) of boys (niños) to girls (niñas) in a club is 3:5. There are 24 girls. How many boys (cuántos niños)?
scaffolds.L3_spanish: La razón de niños a niñas en un club es 3:5. Si hay 24 niñas, ¿cuántos niños hay?
scaffolds.L4_solution:
Step 1: 3/5 = x/24
Step 2: 5x = 72
Step 3: x = 14.4
Answer: approximately 14 boys.

problem_text: A printer can print 240 pages in 6 minutes. How many pages can it print in 45 minutes?

scaffolds.L1_simplified: A printer prints 240 pages in 6 minutes. How many pages in 45 minutes?
scaffolds.L2_bilingual: A printer (impresora) prints (imprime) 240 pages (páginas) in 6 minutes (minutos). How many pages in 45 minutes?
scaffolds.L3_spanish: Una impresora imprime 240 páginas en 6 minutos. ¿Cuántas páginas puede imprimir en 45 minutos?
scaffolds.L4_solution:
Step 1: Rate = 40 pages/min
Step 2: 40 × 45 = 1800
Answer: 1,800 pages.

problem_text: A recipe that serves 4 people requires 2 cups of flour. How many cups of flour are needed to serve 10 people?

scaffolds.L1_simplified: A recipe for 4 people needs 2 cups of flour. How much flour for 10 people?
scaffolds.L2_bilingual: A recipe (receta) for 4 people (personas) needs 2 cups (tazas) of flour (harina). For 10 people, how many cups (cuántas tazas)?
scaffolds.L3_spanish: Una receta para 4 personas necesita 2 tazas de harina. ¿Cuántas tazas se necesitan para 10 personas?

problem_text: A factory produces 3,500 units per week, working 5 days a week and 8 hours per day. What is the production rate per hour?

scaffolds.L1_simplified: A factory makes 3,500 units per week. It works 5 days a week, 8 hours each day. How many units per hour?
scaffolds.L2_bilingual: A factory (fábrica) produces (produce) 3,500 units (unidades) per week (semana), 5 days (días) a week, 8 hours (horas) per day. What is the rate per hour (por hora)?
scaffolds.L3_spanish: Una fábrica produce 3,500 unidades por semana, trabajando 5 días a la semana y 8 horas al día. ¿Cuál es la tasa por hora?

problem_text: The ratio of red to blue marbles in a jar is 4:7. There are 77 marbles in total. How many red marbles are there?

scaffolds.L1_simplified: Red and blue marbles are in a 4:7 ratio. There are 77 total. How many are red?
scaffolds.L2_bilingual: The ratio (razón) of red (rojas) to blue (azules) marbles (canicas) is 4:7. There are 77 total. How many red (cuántas rojas)?
scaffolds.L3_spanish: La razón de canicas rojas a azules es 4:7. Hay 77 en total. ¿Cuántas canicas rojas hay?
scaffolds.L4_solution:
Step 1: Total parts = 11
Step 2: 1 part = 77 ÷ 11 = 7
Step 3: Red = 4 × 7 = 28
Answer: 28 red marbles.

problem_text: A cyclist rides at an average speed of 18 kilometers per hour. How long will it take to complete a 63-kilometer route?

scaffolds.L1_simplified: A cyclist goes 18 km per hour. A route is 63 km long. How long does the ride take?
scaffolds.L2_bilingual: A cyclist (ciclista) rides at 18 km per hour (por hora). How long (cuánto tiempo) to complete (completar) a 63-km route (ruta)?
scaffolds.L3_spanish: Un ciclista va a 18 km por hora. ¿Cuánto tiempo tardará en completar una ruta de 63 km?

problem_text: A school spends money on sports and arts in the ratio 5:3. If the school spends $4,800 on sports, how much on arts?

scaffolds.L1_simplified: A school spends on sports and arts in a 5:3 ratio. Sports costs $4,800. How much on arts?
scaffolds.L2_bilingual: A school (escuela) spends money (dinero) on sports (deportes) and arts (artes) in ratio (razón) 5:3. Sports = $4,800. How much on arts (artes)?
scaffolds.L3_spanish: Una escuela gasta en deportes y artes en razón 5:3. Si gasta $4,800 en deportes, ¿cuánto gasta en artes?

problem_text: Two workers can build a wall in 6 days working together. One worker is twice as fast. How many days would the faster worker alone take?

scaffolds.L1_simplified: Two workers build a wall in 6 days. One is twice as fast. How long for the faster worker alone?
scaffolds.L2_bilingual: Two workers (trabajadores) build (construyen) a wall (pared) in 6 days (días) together (juntos). One is twice as fast (el doble de rápido). How many days (cuántos días) for the faster worker alone?
scaffolds.L3_spanish: Dos trabajadores construyen una pared en 6 días juntos. Uno es el doble de rápido. ¿Cuántos días tardaría el más rápido solo?
scaffolds.L4_solution:
Step 1: Combined = 1/6/day
Step 2: 3x = 1/6 → x = 1/18
Step 3: Fast = 2x = 1/9 → 9 days
Answer: 9 days.

problem_text: A store marks up all products by 40% above cost. A customer pays $84 for an item. What was the original cost to the store?

scaffolds.L1_simplified: A store adds 40% to cost. A customer pays $84. What was the original cost?
scaffolds.L2_bilingual: A store marks up (aumenta el precio) all products by 40% above cost (costo). A customer (cliente) pays $84. What was the original cost (costo original)?
scaffolds.L3_spanish: Una tienda aumenta el precio de todos sus productos en un 40%. Un cliente paga $84. ¿Cuál era el costo original?

problem_text: A tank fills at 15 liters per minute and drains at 9 liters per minute. It holds 360 liters. How long to fill from empty?

scaffolds.L1_simplified: A tank fills at 15 L/min and drains at 9 L/min. It holds 360 liters. How long to fill it?
scaffolds.L2_bilingual: A tank (tanque) fills (se llena) at 15 liters (litros) per minute and drains (se vacía) at 9. It holds (capacidad) 360 liters. How long (cuánto tiempo)?
scaffolds.L3_spanish: Un tanque se llena a 15 litros por minuto y se vacía a 9. Tiene capacidad para 360 litros. ¿Cuánto tarda en llenarse desde vacío?
scaffolds.L4_solution:
Step 1: Net rate = 15 - 9 = 6 L/min
Step 2: 360 ÷ 6 = 60
Answer: 60 minutes.

problem_text: A recipe uses sugar and flour in a ratio of 2:5. If a baker uses 14 cups of sugar, how much flour and what is the total cups?

scaffolds.L1_simplified: Sugar and flour are mixed 2:5. A baker uses 14 cups of sugar. How much flour? How many total?
scaffolds.L2_bilingual: A recipe uses sugar (azúcar) and flour (harina) in ratio (razón) 2:5. With 14 cups (tazas) of sugar, how much flour (harina)? Total (total) cups?
scaffolds.L3_spanish: Una receta usa azúcar y harina en razón 2:5. Si se usan 14 tazas de azúcar, ¿cuánta harina? ¿Cuántas tazas en total?
scaffolds.L4_solution:
Step 1: Flour = 14 × 5/2 = 35
Step 2: Total = 14 + 35 = 49
Answer: 35 cups flour, 49 total.

problem_text: An investment of $5,000 earns simple interest at an annual rate of 6%. How much interest is earned in 3 years, and what is the total value?

scaffolds.L1_simplified: $5,000 is invested at 6% per year. How much interest after 3 years? What is the total?
scaffolds.L2_bilingual: An investment (inversión) of $5,000 earns (genera) 6% interest (interés) per year (por año). How much interest (cuánto interés) in 3 years? Total value (valor total)?
scaffolds.L3_spanish: Una inversión de $5,000 genera el 6% de interés anual. ¿Cuánto interés se gana en 3 años? ¿Cuál es el valor total?

problem_text: Class A collects 3/8 of the total food and Class B collects 5/12. What fraction is left for other classes?

scaffolds.L1_simplified: Class A collects 3/8 and Class B collects 5/12. What fraction is left?
scaffolds.L2_bilingual: Class A (Clase A) collects (recolecta) 3/8 of total (del total). Class B collects 5/12. What fraction (fracción) is left (queda)?
scaffolds.L3_spanish: La Clase A recolecta 3/8 del total y la Clase B 5/12. ¿Qué fracción queda para otras clases?
scaffolds.L4_solution:
Step 1: LCD = 24
Step 2: 9/24 + 10/24 = 19/24
Step 3: Remaining = 1 - 19/24 = 5/24
Answer: 5/24

problem_text: A train travels from City A to City B, a distance of 420 km, in 3 hours and 30 minutes. What is the average speed?

scaffolds.L1_simplified: A train travels 420 km in 3 hours and 30 minutes. What is the average speed?
scaffolds.L2_bilingual: A train (tren) travels (viaja) 420 km in 3 hours (horas) and 30 minutes (minutos). What is the average speed (velocidad promedio)?
scaffolds.L3_spanish: Un tren viaja 420 km en 3 horas y 30 minutos. ¿Cuál es la velocidad promedio?

problem_text: Almonds cost $8 per pound and cashews cost $12 per pound. A 5-pound mixture costs $52. How many pounds of each?

scaffolds.L1_simplified: Almonds cost $8/lb and cashews $12/lb. A 5-pound mix costs $52. How many pounds of each?
scaffolds.L2_bilingual: Almonds (almendras) cost $8 per pound (libra) and cashews (marañones) cost $12. A 5-pound mix (mezcla) costs $52. How many pounds (libras) of each?
scaffolds.L3_spanish: Las almendras cuestan $8 por libra y los marañones $12. Una mezcla de 5 libras cuesta $52. ¿Cuántas libras de cada uno?
scaffolds.L4_solution:
Step 1: a + c = 5; 8a + 12c = 52
Step 2: c = 3, a = 2
Answer: 2 lb almonds, 3 lb cashews.

problem_text: A phone plan charges $25 per month plus $0.10 per text message. A customer's bill is $37.50. How many texts were sent?

scaffolds.L1_simplified: A phone plan costs $25 per month plus $0.10 per text. A bill is $37.50. How many texts?
scaffolds.L2_bilingual: A phone plan (plan) charges (cobra) $25 per month (por mes) plus $0.10 per text (mensaje). A bill (cuenta) is $37.50. How many texts (mensajes)?
scaffolds.L3_spanish: Un plan de teléfono cobra $25 por mes más $0.10 por mensaje. La cuenta es $37.50. ¿Cuántos mensajes se enviaron?
scaffolds.L4_solution:
Step 1: Text cost = $37.50 - $25 = $12.50
Step 2: $12.50 ÷ $0.10 = 125
Answer: 125 messages.

problem_text: A team won 60% of their 35 games this season. How many games did they win and how many did they lose?

scaffolds.L1_simplified: A team won 60% of 35 games. How many did they win? How many did they lose?
scaffolds.L2_bilingual: A team (equipo) won (ganó) 60% of their 35 games (juegos) this season (temporada). How many wins (victorias) and losses (derrotas)?
scaffolds.L3_spanish: Un equipo ganó el 60% de sus 35 juegos. ¿Cuántos ganaron y cuántos perdieron?
scaffolds.L4_solution:
Step 1: Wins = 60% × 35 = 21
Step 2: Losses = 35 - 21 = 14
Answer: 21 wins, 14 losses.

problem_text: Sarah is twice as old as her brother. In 5 years, the sum of their ages will be 37. How old is each now?

scaffolds.L1_simplified: Sarah is twice as old as her brother. In 5 years their ages add up to 37. How old are they now?
scaffolds.L2_bilingual: Sarah is twice as old (el doble de mayor) as her brother (hermano). In 5 years (en 5 años), the sum (suma) of their ages (edades) will be 37. How old (cuántos años) now?
scaffolds.L3_spanish: Sarah tiene el doble de la edad de su hermano. En 5 años, la suma de sus edades será 37. ¿Cuántos años tiene cada uno ahora?
scaffolds.L4_solution:
Step 1: b + 2b + 10 = 37
Step 2: 3b = 27, b = 9
Step 3: s = 18
Answer: Sarah 18, brother 9.

problem_text: A plumber charges a fixed fee of $45 plus $60 per hour. A customer is charged $225. How many hours did the plumber work?

scaffolds.L1_simplified: A plumber charges $45 plus $60 per hour. A customer pays $225. How many hours did he work?
scaffolds.L2_bilingual: A plumber (plomero) charges (cobra) $45 fixed plus (más) $60 per hour (por hora). A customer (cliente) pays $225. How many hours (cuántas horas)?
scaffolds.L3_spanish: Un plomero cobra $45 fijo más $60 por hora. Un cliente paga $225. ¿Cuántas horas trabajó?
scaffolds.L4_solution:
Step 1: 45 + 60h = 225
Step 2: 60h = 180
Step 3: h = 3
Answer: 3 hours.

problem_text: Two numbers add up to 48. The larger number is 3 times the smaller. What are the two numbers?

scaffolds.L1_simplified: Two numbers add up to 48. The larger is 3 times the smaller. What are they?
scaffolds.L2_bilingual: Two numbers (números) add up (suman) to 48. The larger (el mayor) is 3 times (veces) the smaller (el menor). What are they?
scaffolds.L3_spanish: Dos números suman 48. El mayor es 3 veces el menor. ¿Cuáles son los dos números?
scaffolds.L4_solution:
Step 1: s + 3s = 48
Step 2: 4s = 48, s = 12
Step 3: l = 36
Answer: 12 and 36.

problem_text: A cab charges $2.50 for the first mile and $1.75 for each additional mile. A passenger pays $15.25. How many miles did they travel?

scaffolds.L1_simplified: A cab charges $2.50 for the first mile and $1.75 per mile after. A passenger pays $15.25. How many miles?
scaffolds.L2_bilingual: A cab (taxi) charges (cobra) $2.50 for the first mile (primera milla) and $1.75 for each additional (adicional) mile. A passenger (pasajero) pays $15.25. How many miles (millas)?
scaffolds.L3_spanish: Un taxi cobra $2.50 por la primera milla y $1.75 por cada milla adicional. Un pasajero paga $15.25. ¿Cuántas millas recorrió?

problem_text: The sum of three consecutive even numbers is 78. What are the three numbers?

scaffolds.L1_simplified: Three even numbers in a row add up to 78. What are the three numbers?
scaffolds.L2_bilingual: The sum (suma) of three consecutive (consecutivos) even numbers (números pares) is 78. What are the three numbers?
scaffolds.L3_spanish: La suma de tres números pares consecutivos es 78. ¿Cuáles son los tres números?

problem_text: Orange juice costs $3.20 per bottle and apple juice costs $2.80. A customer buys 8 bottles for $24.00. How many of each?

scaffolds.L1_simplified: Orange juice is $3.20 and apple juice is $2.80. 8 bottles cost $24. How many of each?
scaffolds.L2_bilingual: Orange juice (jugo de naranja) costs $3.20 per bottle (botella) and apple juice (jugo de manzana) costs $2.80. 8 bottles cost $24. How many of each (cuántas de cada)?
scaffolds.L3_spanish: El jugo de naranja cuesta $3.20 por botella y el de manzana $2.80. Un cliente compra 8 botellas por $24. ¿Cuántas de cada tipo?
scaffolds.L4_solution:
Step 1: o + a = 8; 3.20o + 2.80a = 24
Step 2: Solve → o = 4, a = 4
Answer: 4 orange, 4 apple.

problem_text: Train A travels north at 80 mph and Train B travels south at 95 mph from the same station. After how many hours are they 525 miles apart?

scaffolds.L1_simplified: Train A goes north at 80 mph and Train B goes south at 95 mph. When are they 525 miles apart?
scaffolds.L2_bilingual: Train A (Tren A) goes north (norte) at 80 mph and Train B south (sur) at 95 mph. After how many hours (cuántas horas) are they 525 miles apart (separados)?
scaffolds.L3_spanish: El Tren A va al norte a 80 mph y el Tren B al sur a 95 mph. ¿Después de cuántas horas estarán a 525 millas de distancia?
scaffolds.L4_solution:
Step 1: Combined = 80 + 95 = 175 mph
Step 2: 525 ÷ 175 = 3
Answer: 3 hours.

problem_text: A savings account earns 4% simple interest per year. After 3 years, the account has $1,120. What was the original deposit?

scaffolds.L1_simplified: A savings account earns 4% per year. After 3 years it has $1,120. How much was deposited?
scaffolds.L2_bilingual: A savings account (cuenta de ahorros) earns (genera) 4% interest (interés) per year. After 3 years it has $1,120. What was the original deposit (depósito original)?
scaffolds.L3_spanish: Una cuenta de ahorros genera el 4% de interés anual. Después de 3 años tiene $1,120. ¿Cuánto se depositó originalmente?

problem_text: A farmer has chickens and cows. There are 20 animals and 56 legs in total. How many chickens and how many cows?

scaffolds.L1_simplified: A farmer has chickens and cows. 20 animals total, 56 legs total. How many chickens? How many cows?
scaffolds.L2_bilingual: A farmer (granjero) has chickens (gallinas) and cows (vacas). 20 animals (animales), 56 legs (patas). How many chickens (gallinas) and cows (vacas)?
scaffolds.L3_spanish: Un granjero tiene gallinas y vacas. Hay 20 animales y 56 patas en total. ¿Cuántas gallinas y cuántas vacas?
scaffolds.L4_solution:
Step 1: c + k = 20; 2c + 4k = 56
Step 2: k = 8, c = 12
Answer: 12 chickens, 8 cows.

problem_text: A movie theater charges $9 for adults and $6 for children. On Saturday, 200 tickets were sold for $1,500 total. How many adult and children's tickets?

scaffolds.L1_simplified: Tickets cost $9 for adults and $6 for children. 200 tickets sold for $1,500. How many of each?
scaffolds.L2_bilingual: A theater charges (cobra) $9 for adults (adultos) and $6 for children (niños). 200 tickets (boletos) sold for $1,500. How many adult (adultos) and children's (niños) tickets?
scaffolds.L3_spanish: Un cine cobra $9 por adulto y $6 por niño. Se vendieron 200 boletos por $1,500. ¿Cuántos boletos de adulto y cuántos de niño?
scaffolds.L4_solution:
Step 1: a + c = 200; 9a + 6c = 1500
Step 2: c = 100, a = 100
Answer: 100 adult, 100 children.

problem_text: The length of a rectangle is 5 cm more than its width. If the area is 84 square centimeters, find the dimensions.

scaffolds.L1_simplified: A rectangle's length is 5 cm more than its width. The area is 84 sq cm. Find the width and length.
scaffolds.L2_bilingual: The length (largo) is 5 cm more than the width (ancho). The area (área) is 84 square centimeters (centímetros cuadrados). Find the dimensions (dimensiones).
scaffolds.L3_spanish: El largo de un rectángulo es 5 cm más que su ancho. Si el área es 84 cm², encuentra las dimensiones.
scaffolds.L4_solution:
Step 1: w(w+5) = 84
Step 2: w² + 5w - 84 = 0
Step 3: w = 7, l = 12
Answer: 7 cm × 12 cm.

problem_text: A ladder 13 feet long leans against a wall. The base is 5 feet from the wall. How high up the wall does it reach?

scaffolds.L1_simplified: A ladder is 13 feet long. Its base is 5 feet from the wall. How high does it reach?
scaffolds.L2_bilingual: A ladder (escalera) 13 feet (pies) long leans against (apoya en) a wall (pared). The base (base) is 5 feet from the wall. How high (qué tan alto)?
scaffolds.L3_spanish: Una escalera de 13 pies se apoya contra una pared. La base está a 5 pies. ¿A qué altura llega?
scaffolds.L4_solution:
Step 1: 5² + h² = 13²
Step 2: h² = 169 - 25 = 144
Step 3: h = 12
Answer: 12 feet.

problem_text: Seven students scored: 72, 85, 91, 68, 85, 77, and 94. Calculate the mean, median, and mode.

scaffolds.L1_simplified: Seven scores: 72, 85, 91, 68, 85, 77, 94. Find the mean, median, and mode.
scaffolds.L2_bilingual: Seven students' scores (calificaciones): 72, 85, 91, 68, 85, 77, 94. Calculate mean (promedio), median (mediana), and mode (moda).
scaffolds.L3_spanish: Siete estudiantes obtuvieron: 72, 85, 91, 68, 85, 77 y 94. Calcula el promedio, la mediana y la moda.
scaffolds.L4_solution:
Step 1: Mean = 572/7 ≈ 81.7
Step 2: Median = 85
Step 3: Mode = 85
Answer: Mean≈81.7, Median=85, Mode=85.

problem_text: A cylindrical water tank has a radius of 3 meters and a height of 10 meters. What is the volume? Use π ≈ 3.14.

scaffolds.L1_simplified: A cylinder has radius 3 m and height 10 m. Find the volume. Use π = 3.14.
scaffolds.L2_bilingual: A cylindrical (cilíndrico) tank has radius (radio) 3 m and height (altura) 10 m. What is the volume (volumen)? Use π ≈ 3.14.
scaffolds.L3_spanish: Un tanque cilíndrico tiene radio 3 m y altura 10 m. ¿Cuál es el volumen? Usa π ≈ 3.14.
scaffolds.L4_solution:
Step 1: V = π × r² × h
Step 2: = 3.14 × 9 × 10 = 282.6
Answer: 282.6 m³.

problem_text: A linear function has a slope of 3 and passes through the point (2, 7). What is the equation of the line and the y-intercept?

scaffolds.L1_simplified: A line has slope 3 and passes through (2, 7). Find the equation and y-intercept.
scaffolds.L2_bilingual: A linear function (función lineal) has slope (pendiente) 3 and passes through (pasa por) point (punto) (2,7). What is the equation (ecuación) and y-intercept (intercepto)?
scaffolds.L3_spanish: Una función lineal tiene pendiente 3 y pasa por el punto (2, 7). ¿Cuál es la ecuación y el intercepto en y?
scaffolds.L4_solution:
Step 1: y - 7 = 3(x - 2)
Step 2: y = 3x + 1
Answer: y = 3x + 1, y-intercept = 1.

problem_text: A bag contains 4 red, 3 blue, and 5 green marbles. One marble is chosen at random. What is the probability of selecting a red or green marble?

scaffolds.L1_simplified: A bag has 4 red, 3 blue, and 5 green marbles. What is the probability of picking red or green?
scaffolds.L2_bilingual: A bag (bolsa) has 4 red (rojas), 3 blue (azules), and 5 green (verdes) marbles. What is the probability (probabilidad) of selecting (elegir) red or green?
scaffolds.L3_spanish: Una bolsa tiene 4 canicas rojas, 3 azules y 5 verdes. ¿Cuál es la probabilidad de elegir una roja o verde?
scaffolds.L4_solution:
Step 1: Total = 12
Step 2: Red+Green = 9
Step 3: P = 9/12 = 3/4
Answer: 3/4.

problem_text: A rectangular field measures 40 meters by 30 meters. What is the length of the diagonal?

scaffolds.L1_simplified: A rectangular field is 40 m long and 30 m wide. What is the diagonal?
scaffolds.L2_bilingual: A rectangular field (campo rectangular) measures 40 meters (metros) by 30 meters. What is the diagonal (diagonal)?
scaffolds.L3_spanish: Un campo rectangular mide 40 metros por 30 metros. ¿Cuánto mide la diagonal?
scaffolds.L4_solution:
Step 1: d² = 40² + 30² = 2500
Step 2: d = √2500 = 50
Answer: 50 meters.

problem_text: The cost to rent a car is $35 per day plus $0.15 per mile driven. On a 3-day trip with 280 miles driven, what was the total rental cost?

scaffolds.L1_simplified: Renting a car costs $35 per day plus $0.15 per mile. 3 days and 280 miles. What is the total?
scaffolds.L2_bilingual: Renting (alquilar) a car costs $35 per day (por día) plus $0.15 per mile (por milla). A 3-day trip with 280 miles. Total cost (costo total)?
scaffolds.L3_spanish: Alquilar un carro cuesta $35 por día más $0.15 por milla. En un viaje de 3 días con 280 millas, ¿cuál fue el costo total?
scaffolds.L4_solution:
Step 1: Daily = 35 × 3 = $105
Step 2: Mileage = 0.15 × 280 = $42
Step 3: Total = $147
Answer: $147.

problem_text: Two dice are rolled simultaneously. What is the probability that the sum equals 8?

scaffolds.L1_simplified: Two dice are rolled. How many ways give sum = 8? What is the probability?
scaffolds.L2_bilingual: Two dice (dados) are rolled (lanzados). What is the probability (probabilidad) that the sum (suma) equals 8?
scaffolds.L3_spanish: Se lanzan dos dados. ¿Cuál es la probabilidad de que la suma sea 8?
scaffolds.L4_solution:
Step 1: Total = 36
Step 2: Pairs summing to 8 = 5
Step 3: P = 5/36
Answer: 5/36.

problem_text: A cone has a base radius of 6 cm and a height of 8 cm. What is the volume? Use π ≈ 3.14.

scaffolds.L1_simplified: A cone has radius 6 cm and height 8 cm. Find the volume. π = 3.14.
scaffolds.L2_bilingual: A cone (cono) has base radius (radio) 6 cm and height (altura) 8 cm. What is the volume (volumen)? Use π ≈ 3.14.
scaffolds.L3_spanish: Un cono tiene radio 6 cm y altura 8 cm. ¿Cuál es el volumen? Usa π ≈ 3.14.

problem_text: A boat travels 24 miles downstream in 2 hours and returns upstream in 3 hours. Find the speed of the boat in still water and the current speed.

scaffolds.L1_simplified: A boat goes 24 miles downstream in 2 hours and back upstream in 3 hours. Find the boat's speed and the current's speed.
scaffolds.L2_bilingual: A boat (bote) travels 24 miles (millas) downstream (río abajo) in 2 hours and returns upstream (río arriba) in 3 hours. Find the speed (velocidad) of the boat and current (corriente).
scaffolds.L3_spanish: Un bote viaja 24 millas río abajo en 2 horas y regresa río arriba en 3 horas. ¿Cuál es la velocidad del bote y la de la corriente?
scaffolds.L4_solution:
Step 1: b+c = 12; b-c = 8
Step 2: 2b = 20 → b = 10
Step 3: c = 2
Answer: 10 mph boat, 2 mph current.

problem_text: In a class of 30 students, 18 play soccer, 12 play basketball, and 6 play both sports. How many students play neither sport?

scaffolds.L1_simplified: 30 students: 18 play soccer, 12 play basketball, 6 play both. How many play neither?
scaffolds.L2_bilingual: In a class (clase) of 30 students, 18 play soccer (fútbol), 12 play basketball (baloncesto), and 6 play both (ambos). How many play neither (ninguno)?
scaffolds.L3_spanish: En una clase de 30 estudiantes, 18 juegan fútbol, 12 baloncesto y 6 ambos. ¿Cuántos no juegan ninguno?
scaffolds.L4_solution:
Step 1: Soccer only=12, Basketball only=6, Both=6
Step 2: Total playing = 24
Step 3: Neither = 6
Answer: 6 students.

problem_text: A frustum (truncated cone) has a bottom radius of 8 cm, top radius of 4 cm, and height of 6 cm. Calculate its volume. Use π ≈ 3.14.

scaffolds.L1_simplified: A truncated cone has bottom radius 8 cm, top radius 4 cm, height 6 cm. Find volume. π = 3.14.
scaffolds.L2_bilingual: A frustum (tronco de cono) has bottom radius (radio inferior) 8 cm, top radius (superior) 4 cm, height (altura) 6 cm. Volume (volumen)? Use π ≈ 3.14.
scaffolds.L3_spanish: Un tronco de cono tiene radio inferior 8 cm, radio superior 4 cm y altura 6 cm. Calcula su volumen. Usa π ≈ 3.14.

problem_text: A chemist prepares 200 mL of a 30% acid solution by mixing a 20% and a 50% acid solution. How many mL of each are needed?

scaffolds.L1_simplified: A chemist mixes a 20% and a 50% acid solution to make 200 mL of a 30% solution. How many mL of each?
scaffolds.L2_bilingual: A chemist (químico) prepares (prepara) 200 mL of a 30% acid (ácido) solution (solución) by mixing (mezclando) a 20% and 50% solution. How many mL of each?
scaffolds.L3_spanish: Un químico prepara 200 mL de solución al 30% mezclando una al 20% y otra al 50%. ¿Cuántos mL de cada una?

problem_text: A box has 5 red, 4 blue, and 3 green balls. Two balls are drawn without replacement. What is the probability both are red?

scaffolds.L1_simplified: A box has 5 red, 4 blue, and 3 green balls. Two are drawn without replacement. What is the probability both are red?
scaffolds.L2_bilingual: A box (caja) has 5 red (rojas), 4 blue (azules), 3 green (verdes) balls. Two are drawn without replacement (sin reemplazo). Probability (probabilidad) both red?
scaffolds.L3_spanish: Una caja tiene 5 pelotas rojas, 4 azules y 3 verdes. Se sacan dos sin reemplazo. ¿Cuál es la probabilidad de que ambas sean rojas?
scaffolds.L4_solution:
Step 1: P(1st red) = 5/12
Step 2: P(2nd red) = 4/11
Step 3: P(both) = 20/132 = 5/33
Answer: 5/33.

problem_text: Pipe A fills a tank in 4 hours. Pipe B fills it in 6 hours. A drain empties it in 12 hours. With all three open, how long to fill the tank?

scaffolds.L1_simplified: Pipe A fills a tank in 4 hours. Pipe B in 6 hours. A drain empties it in 12 hours. With all three open, how long to fill it?
scaffolds.L2_bilingual: Pipe A (tubería A) fills (llena) the tank in 4 hours. Pipe B in 6. A drain (desagüe) empties (vacía) it in 12. With all three open (abiertos), how long (cuánto tiempo)?
scaffolds.L3_spanish: La tubería A llena el tanque en 4 horas, la B en 6. Un desagüe lo vacía en 12. Con los tres abiertos, ¿cuánto tarda en llenarse?
scaffolds.L4_solution:
Step 1: Net rate = 1/4+1/6-1/12 = 4/12 = 1/3
Step 2: Time = 3 hours
Answer: 3 hours.

problem_text: A surveyor walks 60 meters along a riverbank. She measures the angle to a point directly across the river at 35°. What is the width of the river? (tan 35° ≈ 0.7002)

scaffolds.L1_simplified: A surveyor walks 60 meters along a bank and measures a 35° angle across the river. How wide is the river? (tan 35° ≈ 0.7002)
scaffolds.L2_bilingual: A surveyor (topógrafa) walks 60 meters along a riverbank (orilla). She measures the angle (ángulo) across the river (río) at 35°. Width (ancho)? (tan 35° ≈ 0.7002)
scaffolds.L3_spanish: Una topógrafa camina 60 metros a lo largo de una orilla y mide un ángulo de 35° al otro lado. ¿Cuál es el ancho del río? (tan 35° ≈ 0.7002)
scaffolds.L4_solution:
Step 1: Width = 60 × tan(35°)
Step 2: = 60 × 0.7002 ≈ 42
Answer: 42 meters.

problem_text: An investor places part of $10,000 in savings earning 4% annually and the remainder in stocks earning 9%. Total interest after one year is $650. How much in each?

scaffolds.L1_simplified: Part of $10,000 earns 4% and the rest earns 9%. Total interest is $650. How much at each rate?
scaffolds.L2_bilingual: An investor (inversionista) places part of $10,000 in savings (ahorros) at 4% and the rest in stocks (acciones) at 9%. Total interest (interés) after one year: $650. How much in each?
scaffolds.L3_spanish: Un inversionista coloca parte de $10,000 en ahorros al 4% y el resto en acciones al 9%. El interés total es $650. ¿Cuánto en cada uno?

problem_text: A dataset has values: 15, 22, 18, 30, 25, 20, 28, 17, 35, and 10. Calculate the range, variance, and standard deviation.

scaffolds.L1_simplified: 10 values: 15, 22, 18, 30, 25, 20, 28, 17, 35, 10. Find the range, variance, and standard deviation.
scaffolds.L2_bilingual: A dataset (conjunto de datos) has values (valores): 15, 22, 18, 30, 25, 20, 28, 17, 35, 10. Calculate range (rango), variance (varianza), and standard deviation (desviación estándar).
scaffolds.L3_spanish: Un conjunto de datos: 15, 22, 18, 30, 25, 20, 28, 17, 35, 10. Calcula el rango, la varianza y la desviación estándar.
scaffolds.L4_solution:
Step 1: Range = 25
Step 2: Mean = 22
Step 3: Variance ≈ 48.9, SD ≈ 7.0
Answer: Range=25, SD≈7.0.

problem_text: Two cyclists leave the same point in opposite directions at 14 mph and 16 mph. After how many hours are they 135 miles apart? How far has each traveled?

scaffolds.L1_simplified: Two cyclists go in opposite directions at 14 and 16 mph. When are they 135 miles apart? How far has each gone?
scaffolds.L2_bilingual: Two cyclists (ciclistas) go in opposite directions (direcciones opuestas) at 14 mph and 16 mph. After how many hours (cuántas horas) are they 135 miles apart? How far (cuánto) each?
scaffolds.L3_spanish: Dos ciclistas parten en direcciones opuestas a 14 y 16 mph. ¿Cuándo estarán a 135 millas? ¿Cuánto ha recorrido cada uno?
scaffolds.L4_solution:
Step 1: Combined = 30 mph
Step 2: Time = 135/30 = 4.5 hours
Step 3: A=63, B=72
Answer: 4.5 hours.

problem_text: A publisher's cost per book is $12, and fixed monthly costs are $3,600. The book sells for $21. At 400 books sold, what is the monthly profit?

scaffolds.L1_simplified: A book costs $12 to make and sells for $21. Fixed costs are $3,600/month. If 400 books are sold, what is the profit?
scaffolds.L2_bilingual: A publisher's (editorial) cost per book (por libro) is $12. Fixed monthly costs (costos fijos) are $3,600. The book sells (vende) for $21. At 400 books, what is the profit (ganancia)?
scaffolds.L3_spanish: El costo por libro es $12 y los costos fijos mensuales son $3,600. El libro se vende a $21. Si se venden 400 libros, ¿cuál es la ganancia?
scaffolds.L4_solution:
Step 1: Revenue = $21 × 400 = $8,400
Step 2: Variable = $12 × 400 = $4,800
Step 3: Profit = $8,400 - $4,800 - $3,600 = $0
Answer: Break-even, profit = $0.

problem_text: A drug is effective for 340 out of 500 patients. Construct a 95% confidence interval for the true proportion. (Use z = 1.96)

scaffolds.L1_simplified: A drug works for 340 out of 500 patients. Build a 95% confidence interval. Use z = 1.96.
scaffolds.L2_bilingual: A drug is effective (efectivo) for 340 of 500 patients (pacientes). Construct (construir) a 95% confidence interval (intervalo de confianza). Use z = 1.96.
scaffolds.L3_spanish: Un medicamento es efectivo para 340 de 500 pacientes. Construye un intervalo de confianza del 95%. Usa z = 1.96.

problem_text: A ball is launched upward with initial velocity 40 m/s from 5 m height. Its height is h = -5t² + 40t + 5. Find the maximum height and when it occurs.

scaffolds.L1_simplified: A ball's height is h = -5t² + 40t + 5. When does it reach maximum height and how high?
scaffolds.L2_bilingual: A ball (pelota) is launched upward with height (altura) h = -5t² + 40t + 5 meters after t seconds. Find maximum height (altura máxima) and when (cuándo).
scaffolds.L3_spanish: Una pelota se lanza hacia arriba. Su altura es h = -5t² + 40t + 5 metros después de t segundos. ¿Cuál es la altura máxima y cuándo ocurre?

problem_text: A semicircular window with rectangular base has total perimeter 600 cm (including base). Find the radius r that maximizes the window's area.

scaffolds.L1_simplified: A window is a rectangle with a semicircle on top. Total perimeter is 600 cm. Find the radius that maximizes area.
scaffolds.L2_bilingual: A semicircular (semicircular) window with rectangular base (base rectangular). Total perimeter (perímetro total) = 600 cm. Find radius (radio) r that maximizes (maximiza) area (área).
scaffolds.L3_spanish: Una ventana semicircular con base rectangular tiene perímetro total de 600 cm. Encuentra el radio que maximiza el área.
scaffolds.L4_solution:
Step 1: 2r + πr = 600
Step 2: r = 600/(2+π)
Step 3: r ≈ 116.7 cm
Answer: r ≈ 116.7 cm.

problem_text: A bacteria population doubles every 3 hours. Starting with 500 bacteria, write a formula for population after t hours and find when it exceeds 1,000,000.

scaffolds.L1_simplified: Bacteria double every 3 hours. Start with 500. Write the formula and find when they exceed 1,000,000.
scaffolds.L2_bilingual: A bacteria population (población) doubles (se duplica) every 3 hours. Starting (empezando) with 500, write a formula (fórmula) and find when (cuándo) it exceeds (supera) 1,000,000.
scaffolds.L3_spanish: Una población de bacterias se duplica cada 3 horas. Empieza con 500. Escribe la fórmula y determina cuándo supera 1,000,000.
scaffolds.L4_solution:
Step 1: P(t) = 500 × 2^(t/3)
Step 2: 2^(t/3) = 2000
Step 3: t ≈ 32.9 hours
Answer: ≈ 32.9 hours.

problem_text: A company's monthly profit is P(x) = -2x² + 80x - 600 (thousands of dollars), where x is hundreds of units. Find production for maximum profit, the maximum profit, and break-even points.
answer: Max $200k at x=20; break-even x=10, x=30

scaffolds.L1_simplified: Profit P(x) = -2x² + 80x - 600. Find where profit is maximum, the maximum value, and break-even points.
scaffolds.L2_bilingual: A company's profit (ganancia) P(x) = -2x² + 80x - 600 (thousands of dollars), x = hundreds of units (unidades). Find maximum profit (ganancia máxima) and break-even (punto de equilibrio).
scaffolds.L3_spanish: La ganancia mensual es P(x) = -2x² + 80x - 600 (miles de dólares). Encuentra el nivel de producción para máxima ganancia, la ganancia máxima y los puntos de equilibrio.
scaffolds.L4_solution:
Step 1: Max at x = 20
Step 2: P(20) = $200k
Step 3: Break-even: x = 10 and x = 30
Answer: Max $200k at 2000 units.

problem_text: A sample of 36 students using a new teaching method scores mean 78.5 with SD=9. The null hypothesis is mean=75. Calculate the z-score and decide if the method is significantly better at α=0.05. (z-critical=1.645)

scaffolds.L1_simplified: 36 students average 78.5 with SD=9. Null hypothesis: mean=75. Find the z-score and decide if we reject the null at α=0.05.
scaffolds.L2_bilingual: A sample (muestra) of 36 students has mean (media) 78.5 with SD (desviación estándar) = 9. Null hypothesis (hipótesis nula): mean=75. Calculate z-score (puntuación z) and decide (decidir) at α=0.05.
scaffolds.L3_spanish: Una muestra de 36 estudiantes tiene media 78.5 con DE=9. Hipótesis nula: media=75. Calcula el valor z y decide si se rechaza al nivel α=0.05.
scaffolds.L4_solution:
Step 1: z = (78.5-75)/(9/6) = 3.5/1.5 = 2.33
Step 2: 2.33 > 1.645 → Reject null
Answer: z≈2.33, reject null.

problem_text: A company's revenue is R(t) = 50t + 200 and cost is C(t) = t² - 10t + 400 (thousands), where t is years since launch. When does profit first become positive, and when does it peak?

scaffolds.L1_simplified: Revenue R(t) = 50t + 200 and Cost C(t) = t² - 10t + 400. When is profit positive and when does it peak?
scaffolds.L2_bilingual: Revenue (ingresos) R(t) = 50t+200 and cost (costos) C(t) = t²-10t+400 (thousands, t=years). When is the company profitable (rentable)? When does profit peak (alcanza su máximo)?
scaffolds.L3_spanish: Los ingresos son R(t) = 50t+200 y los costos C(t) = t²-10t+400. ¿Cuándo es rentable la empresa y cuándo alcanza su máxima ganancia?
scaffolds.L4_solution:
Step 1: P(t) = -t² + 60t - 200
Step 2: Profitable from t≈3.6
Step 3: Max at t=30: $700k
Answer: Profitable ~year 4, peaks at year 30.

problem_text: A machine produces bolts with diameters normally distributed: mean 10 mm, SD 0.5 mm. A bolt is defective if its diameter is more than 1.5 mm from the mean. What percentage are acceptable, and how many defective in 10,000?

scaffolds.L1_simplified: Bolt diameters: mean 10 mm, SD 0.5 mm. Defective if more than 1.5 mm from mean. What % are good? How many defective in 10,000?
scaffolds.L2_bilingual: A machine produces bolts (pernos) with normal distribution (distribución normal): mean (media) 10 mm, SD (desviación) 0.5 mm. Defective (defectuoso) if more than 1.5 mm from mean. What percentage acceptable (aceptables)? How many defective in 10,000?
scaffolds.L3_spanish: Una máquina produce pernos con distribución normal: media 10 mm, DE 0.5 mm. Defectuoso si difiere más de 1.5 mm. ¿Qué porcentaje es aceptable? ¿Cuántos defectuosos en 10,000?
scaffolds.L4_solution:
Step 1: z = 1.5/0.5 = 3
Step 2: Within 3 SDs = 99.73%
Step 3: Defective = 27
Answer: 99.73% acceptable, ~27 defective.

problem_text: A company's market share follows S(t) = 80 / (1 + 15e^(-0.3t)), where t is years since market entry. Find: initial share, long-term maximum, and when share first exceeds 50%.
answer: Initial 5%, max 80%, exceeds 50% at t≈10.8 years

scaffolds.L1_simplified: Market share follows S(t) = 80/(1+15e^(-0.3t)). Find starting share, maximum long-term share, and when it exceeds 50%.
scaffolds.L2_bilingual: Market share (participación de mercado) S(t) = 80/(1+15e^(-0.3t)), t = years (años). Find initial share (participación inicial), maximum (máximo), and when it exceeds (supera) 50%.
scaffolds.L3_spanish: La participación de mercado sigue S(t) = 80/(1+15e^(-0.3t)). Encuentra la participación inicial, el máximo a largo plazo y cuándo supera el 50%.
scaffolds.L4_solution:
Step 1: S(0) = 80/16 = 5%
Step 2: Max = 80%
Step 3: Exceeds 50% at t ≈ 10.8 years
Answer: 5% initial, 80% max, 50% at ~year 11.
